prompt: I have a CLAUDE.md file for my Java Spring Boot microservices repo and Claude keeps ignoring parts of it. [see full prompt in evals.json]

assertions.text: Identifies that the file is too long / has too many rules as the root cause of Claude ignoring parts
assertions.check: response identifies length, number of rules, or context limit as the root cause

assertions.text: Recommends deleting generic platitudes (e.g., 'write clean readable code', 'follow SOLID principles', 'use meaningful variable names')
assertions.check: response recommends removing at least some of the generic/standard rules

assertions.text: Identifies non-obvious project-specific rules worth keeping (e.g., PostgreSQL for integration tests, Flyway, JPA-not-native-SQL)
assertions.check: response identifies at least one non-obvious rule to keep (integration test DB requirement, Flyway, JPA policy, etc.)

assertions.text: Recommends putting commands (build/test/run) prominently or first
assertions.check: response recommends elevating or prioritizing the commands section

assertions.text: Provides either a revised shorter CLAUDE.md or explicit keep/delete/move guidance for each rule category
assertions.check: response provides actionable guidance (revised template or categorized list), not just abstract advice

assertions.text: Mentions a target size or line count for CLAUDE.md
assertions.check: response mentions a size target (e.g., under 150 lines, under 300 lines, 60 lines, etc.)
